Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 083. Harvest in Hindanu (ABL 0093)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ía

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra. Good health to the king, my lord! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—É.ŠÁR o*

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ía

o 44

aš-šur dNIN.LÍL a-na LUGAL

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-bu

o 66

UD 11-KÁM e-ṣa-du

(6) They finished the harvest in Hin[zanu] on the 11th. [......] was reaped [...]. As long as [I am] here [......

o 77

ina KUR.ḫi-⸢in-za*-ni*⸣

o 88

ú-sa-[li-mu x x]

o 99

e-ṣi-[di x x x]

o 1010

[a]-⸢du* É*⸣ [x x]

o 1111

[an]-na*-⸢ka*⸣ [x x]

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[la]-a*-šú la*-a* [i-ma-gúr]

r 2'2'

la*-a i-⸢za*⸣-[bi-lu]

(r 2) They will not [agree] to trans[port it ...]; they are transporting it on your expense.

r 3'3'

ina li-pa-⸢ni-ka⸣

r 4'4'

i-za-bi-lu

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334042/.
